task_text:
Fix `Approx` matcher not handling subnormal floating-point values correctly

In `matchers/catch_matchers_floating_point.cpp`, the `WithinAbsMatcher` compares using absolute epsilon but does not account for subnormal float values where the default epsilon is larger than the values themselves, causing false positives for comparisons near zero.
solve_notes: The old implementation used `(matchee + m_margin >= m_target) && (m_target + m_margin >= matchee)` — a form originally chosen to avoid `INF - INF = NaN`. However this add-margin form gives false positives for subnormal values because addition at normal margin scale swamps subnormal magnitudes. The fix uses `std::fabs(matchee - m_target) <= m_margin` for all finite inputs (subnormals included), falling back to the add-margin form only when either operand is infinite.

search_sequence.action: Read `WithinAbsMatcher::match` in catch_matchers_floating_point.cpp starting at line 84
search_sequence.result: Found a two-condition add-margin comparison with a comment above it: 'Performs equivalent check of std::fabs(lhs - rhs) <= margin / But without the subtraction to allow for INFINITY in comparison'
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description named the file and matcher type; the comment revealed exactly why the old form existed

search_sequence.action: Read `marginComparison` helper at line 52 of the same file
search_sequence.result: Comment repeats 'without the subtraction to allow for INFINITY'; confirms the only reason to avoid subtraction is INF - INF = NaN
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to verify no other callers relied on the add-margin form before changing it

search_sequence.result: Class stores `m_target` and `m_margin` both `double`; `match` takes `double const&`
search_sequence.reasoning: Confirmed field types so the `std::isinf` guard applied to the right expressions

key_decisions.decision: Guard infinity case with `!std::isinf` on both operands rather than a general special-case function
key_decisions.reasoning: The comment explicitly states the only motivation for add-margin is infinity; a minimal guard on both operands preserves that case while fixing subnormals

exploration_log.aha_moment: The comment 'without the subtraction to allow for INFINITY' confirmed that std::fabs subtraction is safe for all finite values and only needs a fallback for infinities — making the fix straightforward.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have gone directly to the match function without reading marginComparison; the comment on match itself was already conclusive.

minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: replaced add-margin comparison with fabs-subtraction form guarded by isinf check

thrash_preventing_defs.name: WithinAbsMatcher
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to confirm field names m_target and m_margin and their types before writing the guard expression; without this an agent would search for the field declarations

thrash_preventing_defs.name: marginComparison
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the 'without the subtraction to allow for INFINITY' comment confirmed the only reason the add-margin form exists; seeing this upfront prevents an agent from preserving the add-margin form out of caution

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient is just `WithinAbsMatcher::match` — editing it is the entire fix. thrash_preventing adds the class declaration (so the agent knows field types and names without searching) and `marginComparison` (whose comment explains why the old form existed, preventing the agent from over-engineering a more complex fix).

excluded_defs.name: WithinAbsMatcher::WithinAbsMatcher
excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm m_margin validation logic; not needed for the fix itself once field names were confirmed from the header
